title: Isolation and Proteomic Analysis of Plasma-Derived Exosomes in Parkinson’s Disease
author: Wenzheng Hu

steps.title: Extraction of Sample Proteins
steps.instruction: Extraction of Sample Proteins

steps.title: Five milliliters of venous blood were...
steps.instruction: Five milliliters of venous blood were collected from each fasting participant in the morning.

steps.title: The supernatant from step 6 was filtered through a 0
steps.instruction: The supernatant from step 6 was filtered through a 0.22 μm filter into a 10.4 mL ultracentrifuge tube (Thermo Fisher, catalog #010-1382), balanced with PBS, and then ultracentrifuged using a Beckman Optima XPN-90 ultracentrifuge with a Thermo Scientific TM Fiberlite TM F65L‑6 × 13.5 rotor at 150,000×g for 90 minutes at 4°C to obtain an Exo pellet.

steps.title: One milliliter of plasma was transferred into a 1
steps.instruction: One milliliter of plasma was transferred into a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube and centrifuged at 2,500×g for 15 minutes to remove dead cells and cell debris.

steps.title: Separate plasma by centrifugation at 2,500×g...
steps.instruction: Separate plasma by centrifugation at 2,500×g for 15 minutes, carefully avoiding the buffy coat layer.

steps.title: Transfer the plasma supernatant to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the plasma supernatant to a new 10 mL centrifuge tube and centrifuge again at 2,500×g for 15 minutes to remove residual cell debris and remaining platelets.

steps.title: Aliquot the resulting plasma into cryovials...
steps.instruction: Aliquot the resulting plasma into cryovials (1 mL per tube) and store at –80°C until use.

steps.title: The supernatant was transferred into a...
steps.instruction: The supernatant was transferred into a new tube and centrifuged at 17,000×g for 90 minutes at 4°C using a refrigerated centrifuge (Thermo Scientific Fresco 21) to obtain a microvesicles (MVs) pellet.

steps.title: After centrifugation, the supernatant was discarded,...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, the supernatant was discarded, unless exosomes (Exo) are required, in which case the supernatant from this step should be collected for subsequent Exo isolation (see the isolation of exosomes from plasma section). The MVs pellet was then resuspended in 500 μL PBS and centrifuged again at 17,000×g for 90 minutes at 4 °C using a refrigerated centrifuge (Thermo Scientific Fresco 21).

steps.title: The supernatant was aspirated, and the...
steps.instruction: The supernatant was aspirated, and the tube was then inverted on a paper towel for 3–5 minutes to remove residual supernatant.

steps.title: The final MVs pellet was resuspended...
steps.instruction: The final MVs pellet was resuspended in 50–100 μL of PBS or RIPA lysis buffer, depending on downstream applications.

steps.title: After centrifugation, the supernatant was discarded
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, the supernatant was discarded. The tube was then inverted on a paper towel for 3–5 minutes to remove residual supernatant.

steps.title: The Exo pellet was resuspended in...
steps.instruction: The Exo pellet was resuspended in 50 μL of PBS or RIPA buffer, depending on downstream applications.

steps.title: SDS-PAGE (Electrophoresis)
steps.instruction: SDS-PAGE (Electrophoresis)

steps.title: Load the denatured samples onto an 8–16% gradient SDS-polyacrylamide gel
steps.instruction: Load the denatured samples onto an 8–16% gradient SDS-polyacrylamide gel.

steps.title: Run the gel at constant voltage
steps.instruction: Run the gel at constant voltage: 80 V for 10 minutes (stacking gel), then increase to 120 V for 1 hour (separating gel).

steps.title: Protein Transfer to PVDF Membrane
steps.instruction: Protein Transfer to PVDF Membrane

steps.title: Activate PVDF membrane in methanol for...
steps.instruction: Activate PVDF membrane in methanol for 15–30 seconds, then rinse in transfer buffer.

steps.title: Assemble the transfer sandwich (gel against PVDF membrane)
steps.instruction: Assemble the transfer sandwich (gel against PVDF membrane).

steps.title: Transfer proteins to the PVDF membrane...
steps.instruction: Transfer proteins to the PVDF membrane using a wet transfer apparatus at constant current 200 mA for 2 hours.

steps.title: Blocking
steps.instruction: Blocking

steps.title: After transfer, wash the membrane briefly in 1× TBST
steps.instruction: After transfer, wash the membrane briefly in 1× TBST.

steps.title: Block the membrane with 5% skimmed...
steps.instruction: Block the membrane with 5% skimmed milk in 1× TBST at room temperature for 45 minutes with gentle shaking.

steps.title: Primary Antibody Incubation
steps.instruction: Primary Antibody Incubation

steps.title: Dilute primary antibodies in blocking buffer...
steps.instruction: Dilute primary antibodies in blocking buffer or TBST as follows: CD9 (Abcam, ab223052) – 1:2000, CD63 (ImmunoWay, YT5525) – 1:2000, CD81 (Biolegend, 349501) – 1:2000, Actinin-4 (Abcam, ab108198) – 1:2000, APOA1 (Abcam, ab52945) – 1:2000. Incubate the membrane with primary antibody solution overnight at 4°C with gentle shaking.

steps.title: Remove the primary antibody solution
steps.instruction: Remove the primary antibody solution.

steps.title: Wash the membrane with 1× TBST...
steps.instruction: Wash the membrane with 1× TBST for 10 minutes at room temperature with shaking, and repeat this washing step two more times for a total of three 10-minute washes.

steps.title: Prepare instrument
steps.instruction: Prepare instrument

steps.title: Take a small amount of EVs...
steps.instruction: Take a small amount of EVs sample and dilute it 100-fold with PBS (e.g., 3 µL EV + 297 µL PBS).

steps.title: Secondary Antibody Incubation
steps.instruction: Secondary Antibody Incubation

steps.title: Dilute HRP-conjugated secondary antibody (anti-rabbit or...
steps.instruction: Dilute HRP-conjugated secondary antibody (anti-rabbit or anti-mouse, depending on the primary host species) at 1:5000 in 1× TBST, and incubate the membrane with the solution for 2 hours at room temperature with gentle shaking.

steps.title: Remove the secondary antibody solution
steps.instruction: Remove the secondary antibody solution.

steps.title: Wash the membrane three times with...
steps.instruction: Wash the membrane three times with 1× TBST at room temperature with shaking: the first two washes for 10 minutes each, and the third wash for 20 minutes.

steps.title: Signal Detection
steps.instruction: Signal Detection

steps.title: Prepare enhanced chemiluminescence (ECL) substrate according to the manufacturer’s instructions
steps.instruction: Prepare enhanced chemiluminescence (ECL) substrate according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Drain excess liquid from the membrane...
steps.instruction: Drain excess liquid from the membrane (do not let it dry).

steps.title: Apply ECL substrate to the membrane...
steps.instruction: Apply ECL substrate to the membrane and incubate for 1–2 minutes.

steps.title: Visualize chemiluminescent signals using a gel documentation system (e
steps.instruction: Visualize chemiluminescent signals using a gel documentation system (e.g., ChemiDoc).

steps.title: Drop an appropriate volume (e
steps.instruction: Drop an appropriate volume (e.g., 5–10 µL) of the diluted EVs sample onto a copper grid coated with Lacey carbon film.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of 2% phosphotungstic...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of 2% phosphotungstic acid to the grid and incubate for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove excess staining solution and...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove excess staining solution and allow the grid to air-dry completely.

steps.title: Examine the dried grid under a transmission electron microscope (e
steps.instruction: Examine the dried grid under a transmission electron microscope (e.g., Tecnai‑G2‑Spirit‑FEI/Quanta Microscope; Philips, Eindhoven, Netherlands). Capture images for morphological analysis.

steps.title: Sample dilution and loading
steps.instruction: Sample dilution and loading

steps.title: Parameter setting and measurement
steps.instruction: Parameter setting and measurement

steps.title: Calibrate the ZetaView PMX 110 (Particle...
steps.instruction: Calibrate the ZetaView PMX 110 (Particle Metrix, Meerbusch, Germany) using 100 nm polystyrene beads (ThermoFisher Scientific) prior to sample analysis.

steps.title: Dilute each EVs sample 1000‑fold in sterile 0
steps.instruction: Dilute each EVs sample 1000‑fold in sterile 0.22 μm filtered PBS (e.g., 2 µL sample into 1998ul PBS). Mix gently.

steps.title: Set pre‑acquisition parameters
steps.instruction: Set pre‑acquisition parameters: temperature 23°C, sensitivity 70, specificity 70, frame rate 30 fps, shutter speed 100.

steps.title: Analyze data
steps.instruction: Analyze data

steps.title: Use ZetaView software (version 8
steps.instruction: Use ZetaView software (version 8.02.28) and Microsoft Excel 2013 for data analysis.

steps.title: MVs from the PD group and...
steps.instruction: MVs from the PD group and the control group were extracted following the method described in the Isolation of microvesicles from plasma section.

steps.title: Protein Concentration Measurement
steps.instruction: Protein Concentration Measurement

steps.title: Protein concentration for each sample was...
steps.instruction: Protein concentration for each sample was measured using the BCA method as described in the Protein Quantification and Sample Preparation of the Western Blot Analysis of Extracellular Vesicle Markers section.

steps.title: Electrophoresis of Equal Protein Mass Samples
steps.instruction: Electrophoresis of Equal Protein Mass Samples

steps.title: The steps were performed in the...
steps.instruction: The steps were performed in the same manner as described in the Western blot electrophoresis procedure.

steps.title: Filter-Aided Sample Preparation (FASP) for Protein Digestion
steps.instruction: Filter-Aided Sample Preparation (FASP) for Protein Digestion

steps.title: Protein Reduction： Equal amounts of protein...
steps.instruction: Protein Reduction： Equal amounts of protein from each sample were added to 10 kDa ultrafiltration tubes. The sample liquids were replaced with lysis buffer, followed by centrifugation to achieve equal volumes. Dithiothreitol (DTT) was added at a ratio of 40:1 (final DTT concentration 25 mM) relative to the total solution volume. The mixture was vortexed, then incubated in a water bath at 37°C for 1 hour. The samples were cooled to room temperature.

steps.title: Sample Washing :A 5-fold dilution of...
steps.instruction: Sample Washing :A 5-fold dilution of TEAB (300 µl) was added to the samples, followed by centrifugation at 12,000 rpm (13,400 g) for 10 minutes. The solution at the bottom of the collection tube was discarded. This process was repeated three times. Subsequently, a 2-fold dilution of TEAB (300 µl) was added, and the centrifugation step was repeated.

steps.title: Protein Alkylation :Iodoacetamide (IAA) was added...
steps.instruction: Protein Alkylation :Iodoacetamide (IAA) was added to the reduced samples at a solution-to-IAA ratio of 20:1 (final IAA concentration 50 mM). The mixture was vortexed, then incubated at room temperature in the dark for 30 minutes.

steps.title: Enzymatic Digestion in Filter-Aided Solution :The...
steps.instruction: Enzymatic Digestion in Filter-Aided Solution :The ultrafiltration tube was replaced with a new collection tube. Trypsin was added at a protein-to-trypsin ratio of 15:1 and incubated at 37°C overnight for digestion.

steps.title: Freeze-Dried Samples Place the collected eluent...
steps.instruction: Freeze-Dried Samples Place the collected eluent in a rotary vacuum concentrator and vacuum dry it without heating. After drying, store it in a -80°C freezer for future use.

steps.title: Peptide Collection
steps.instruction: Peptide Collection : On the following day, a 2-fold dilution of TEAB (100 µl) was added, followed by centrifugation at 12,000 rpm (13,400 g) for 10 minutes. This process was repeated three times. The peptide solution obtained from enzymatic digestion was collected at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: C18 Solid-Phase Extraction
steps.instruction: C18 Solid-Phase Extraction

steps.title: Activation of the C18 Solid-Phase Extraction...
steps.instruction: Activation of the C18 Solid-Phase Extraction Column :Pipette 100 µL of 100% ACN into the column, centrifuge at 3,000 rpm for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Equilibration of the C18 Solid-Phase Extraction...
steps.instruction: Equilibration of the C18 Solid-Phase Extraction Column :Pipette 100 µL of 50% ACN with 0.1% FA into the column, centrifuge at 3,000 rpm for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Second Equilibration of the C18 Solid-Phase...
steps.instruction: Second Equilibration of the C18 Solid-Phase Extraction Column : Pipette 100 µL of 2% ACN with 0.1% FA into the column, centrifuge at 3,000 rpm for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Sample Loading
steps.instruction: Sample Loading : Redissolve the sample in 50 µL of 0.1% TFA and load it into the column. Centrifuge at 2,700 rpm for 3 minutes, repeating this process three times.

steps.title: Desalting Wash :Pipette 100 µL of 2% ACN with 0
steps.instruction: Desalting Wash :Pipette 100 µL of 2% ACN with 0.1% FA, centrifuge at 3,000 rpm for 3 minutes, and repeat this process twice.

steps.title: Elution :Pipette 50 µL of 50% ACN with 0
steps.instruction: Elution :Pipette 50 µL of 50% ACN with 0.1% FA, collect the eluate into an EP tube, and centrifuge at 2,700 rpm for 3 minutes. Repeat this process once and combine the eluates.

steps.title: Freeze-Dry the Eluate :Place the collected...
steps.instruction: Freeze-Dry the Eluate :Place the collected eluate in a rotary vacuum concentrator and vacuum dry it without heating. After drying, store it in a -80°C freezer for future use.

steps.title: LC-MS/MS Analysis
steps.instruction: LC-MS/MS Analysis

steps.title: Redissolve the peptide fractions in 10 µL of 0
steps.instruction: Redissolve the peptide fractions in 10 µL of 0.1% FA solution.

steps.title: Perform separation using a Vanquish liquid...
steps.instruction: Perform separation using a Vanquish liquid chromatography system with a nanoViper C18 column (75 μm × 250 mm, 2 μm). The mobile phases are as follows:Phase A: 0.1% FA in water, Phase B: 80% acetonitrile with 0.1% FA in water. The effective elution gradient is 1%–35% over a total elution time of 90 minutes, with a flow rate of 0.6 µL/min.The liquid chromatography conditions are as follows: Low-pH Reversed-Phase Chromatography Gradient %s %s

steps.title: DIA Library Search
steps.instruction: DIA Library Search

steps.title: Set search parameters Use human protein database; enzyme
steps.instruction: Set search parameters Use human protein database; enzyme: trypsin (up to 2 missed cleavages); precursor mass tolerance ±10 ppm; fragment mass tolerance ±0.02 Da; fixed modification: carbamidomethylation (C); variable modifications: oxidation (M) and N-terminal acetylation; FDR <1% at peptide and protein level; protein identification requires ≥1 unique peptide.

steps.title: DIA Data Import Import DIA data...
steps.instruction: DIA Data Import Import DIA data into Spectronaut and retain results with a q-value <1.0% for final analysis.

steps.title: Protein Quantification and Sample Preparation
steps.instruction: Protein Quantification and Sample Preparation

steps.title: The protein concentration of each Extracellular...
steps.instruction: The protein concentration of each Extracellular vesicles (EVs) preparation was determined using the micro bicinchoninic acid (BCA) protein assay according to the manufacturer’s instructions (Cat. No. B0010-1, Beyotime).

steps.title: Take 20 µg of protein per sample
steps.instruction: Take 20 µg of protein per sample.

steps.title: Add an appropriate amount of 5×...
steps.instruction: Add an appropriate amount of 5× concentrated buffer to each sample to adjust the final concentration to 1× . Then, add 1× buffer to each sample to equalize the volume of all samples.

steps.title: Heat the samples at 95°C for...
steps.instruction: Heat the samples at 95°C for 10 minutes to denature proteins.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the samples to collect condensation
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the samples to collect condensation.

steps.title: Flush the sample chamber with sterile...
steps.instruction: Flush the sample chamber with sterile PBS that has been filtered through a 0.22 μm filter to remove any residual particles. Ensure the chamber is completely clean before sample loading.

steps.title: Inject the diluted sample into the sample chamber
steps.instruction: Inject the diluted sample into the sample chamber.

steps.title: Measure at 11 different positions, with 3 cycles per position
steps.instruction: Measure at 11 different positions, with 3 cycles per position.

steps.title: Apply post‑acquisition parameters
steps.instruction: Apply post‑acquisition parameters: minimum brightness 25, particle size range 5–1000 nm.

steps.title: Perform mass spectrometry analysis Use an...
steps.instruction: Perform mass spectrometry analysis Use an Orbitrap Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer in high-sensitivity mode. Set full scan (MS1): resolution 120,000, scan range 350–1250 m/z, AGC target 3e6, max injection time 50 ms. Set fragmentation scan (MS2): resolution 30,000, AGC target 1e6, collision energy 25%/30%/35%. Configure DIA with 80 scanning windows. Total scan time: 90 minutes.

steps.title: Process spectral data Use Spectronaut (version 16
steps.instruction: Process spectral data Use Spectronaut (version 16.0.220606.53000) in directDIA mode with the reference database uniprot_human_81803_20230327.fasta. .

steps.title: Wash the membrane with 1× TBST...
steps.instruction: Wash the membrane with 1× TBST for 10 minutes at room temperature with shaking, and repeat this washing step two more times for a total of three 10-minute washes.
